summary: Legendary Help/Slipknot/Franklin run, soaring Comes A Time, tight second set
blurb: The show opens with an incendiary Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin's Tower that sets the night on fire. Jerry’s guitar and vocal work shine throughout, especially on The Music Never Stopped and Comes A Time. The second set delivers peaks with Ship Of Fools, Estimated Prophet, The Other One and a heartfelt Comes A Time finale.
review: May 9, 1977 at Buffalo’s War Memorial Auditorium captures the Grateful Dead in peak ’77 form. From the first note of Help On The Way through the seamless Slipknot transition into Franklin's Tower, the band launches an electric surge that carries the entire evening. Jerry Garcia’s lead guitar is described as “crying,” “all‑time greatest,” and a “glorious mess” on Big River, while his vocal performance on Comes A Time earns tear‑inducing praise. Phil Lesh holds down a rock‑solid bass line that reviewers call “on point,” and Bob Weir stays right behind Jerry, delivering rhythm and harmonies that give listeners “goosebumps.” Keith Godchaux’s piano work is singled out as a favorite, adding melodic sparkle to the jams. The rhythm section of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drives the set with a “phenomenal” post‑drum segment, propelling high‑energy numbers like Ship Of Fools, Estimated Prophet and The Other One. The second‑set climax arrives with an emotional Comes A Time that many call one of the most moving moments in the band’s catalog, before closing the night on Uncle John’s Band. All recordings agree: this is a quintessential 1977 performance, captured with crystal‑clear soundboard fidelity that makes it a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the same standout songs (Help/Slipknot/Franklin sequence, Comes A Time, Ship Of Fools, Estimated Prophet) and Jerry’s guitar/vocals. No negative comments appear across any analysis, indicating consistently strong performance appreciation.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with superior mix, preserving every instrument and the show’s emotional peaks without noticeable dropouts.

key_highlights: Incendiary Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin's Tower opener
Tear‑inducing Comes A Time solo and vocal performance
Second‑set peaks: Ship Of Fools, Estimated Prophet, The Other One
Crystal‑clear soundboard recording that rivals studio quality

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers a volcanic performance—his guitar cries, his solos (Big River, Comes A Time) are described as glorious and all‑time great, with soaring vocals that lift the entire show.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass locks in solidly, providing the steady foundation praised as on‑point throughout the night.
band_performance.Bob: Weir stays right behind Jerry, offering strong rhythm guitar and harmonies; a special intertwining moment gives listeners goosebumps.
band_performance.Keith: Godchaux’s piano adds melodic sparkle and is singled out as a favorite keyboard contribution that lifts the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming drives the energy forward, highlighted by a phenomenal post‑drum segment.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann provides tight, propulsive beats that keep the set’s momentum high.
